content.problemStatement:
You need to publish XXX comic books using YYY printing machines and ZZZ binding machines. Each printing machine has its own printing time, where the iii-th machine takes Print[i]Print[i]Print[i] minutes to print one comic book. After printing, each comic book needs to be bound, and all binding machines take the same time KKK minutes to bind one comic book.

Rules:


A printing/binding machine can process only one comic book at a time
A comic book must be completely printed before it can be bound
There can be any non-negative waiting time between printing and binding
All pages of a comic must be printed on the same machine and bound on the same machine
Find the minimum time needed to completely publish all XXX comic books.


Input Format

First line contains TTT - number of test cases
For each test case:
First line contains four space-separated integers: XXX YYY ZZZ KKK
Second line contains YYY space-separated integers: Print[1]Print[1]Print[1] Print[2]Print[2]Print[2] ... Print[Y]Print[Y]Print[Y]


Output Format

For each test case, print a single integer - the minimum time required to publish all comics

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1:

Need to publish 2 comics using 2 printing machines and 1 binding machine
Printing times: Machine 1 = 2 mins, Machine 2 = 4 mins
Binding time = 3 mins
Optimal sequence:
Comic 1: Print on Machine 1 (0-2), Bind (2-5)
Comic 2: Print on Machine 1 (2-4), Bind (5-8)

Total time = 8 minutes
Test Case 2:

Need to publish 3 comics using 1 printing machine and 2 binding machines
Printing time = 4 mins for the single machine
Binding time = 5 mins
The time needed is 17 minutes
